title: Cloning the Self for Mental Well-Being: A Framework for Designing Safe and Therapeutic Self-Clone Chatbots
one_sentence_summary: The paper investigates the therapeutic potential and risks of self-clone chatbots through interviews with experts and users to propose a framework for safe and responsible design.
research_problem: Self-clone chatbots, which replicate a user's conversational patterns for intra-personal exploration, carry significant risks including identity confusion, negative reinforcement, and blurred user agency.
method_summary: The study utilized in-depth interviews with mental health experts and prospective end-users to identify perceived affordances, ethical implications, and design requirements for digital self-clones.
dataset_or_materials: Interviews conducted with 16 mental health experts (providers and researchers) and 6 prospective end-users.
main_findings: The authors developed a design framework for self-clone chatbots organized around three priorities: grounding the approach in therapeutic models, defining design dimensions, and establishing safety guardrails for mild, non-clinical symptoms.

section_map.heading: Cloning the Self for Mental Well-Being: A Framework for Designing Safe and Therapeutic Self-Clone Chatbots
